title: Getting started with spellbind
text: brand three primary colours — purple, blue, rose — plus set neutrals tints. rladies_cols() front door: Call arguments see everything:
code: library(spellbind)  rladies_cols("purple", "blue", "rose") #>    purple      blue      rose  #> "#881ef9" "#146af9" "#ff5b92" rladies_cols() #>         purple   purple_light      purple_50 purple_lighter    purple_dark  #>      "#881ef9"      "#a152f8"      "#bb86f7"      "#d4b9f5"      "#6b0fd4"  #>  purple_darker           blue        blue_75        blue_50        blue_25  #>      "#5009a8"      "#146af9"      "#4a8bf8"      "#80acf6"      "#b7ccf5"  #>           rose        rose_75        rose_50        rose_25       charcoal  #>      "#ff5b92"      "#fb80ab"      "#f6a4c3"      "#f2c9dc"      "#2f2f30"  #>    charcoal_75    charcoal_50    charcoal_25       lavender        surface  #>      "#5f5f61"      "#8e8e92"      "#bebec3"      "#ededf4"      "#f8f8fc"  #>    surface_alt  #>      "#f3f0fa"

title: Getting started with spellbind
text: scales drop straight ggplot2 code. scale_colour_rladies() scale_fill_rladies() handle discrete data; _c variants handle continuous. Discrete colour scale applied species. continuous data, pick single-hue palette like "purple" "blue": Continuous fill scale using purple palette.
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2.5) +   scale_colour_rladies() +   theme_rladies() +   labs(title = "Iris sepal dimensions") ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_rladies_c("purple") +   theme_rladies() +   labs(title = "Old Faithful eruptions")

title: Getting started with spellbind
text: Nine palettes ship package. serves different purpose: available palettes glance. rladies_pal() returns function, ggplot2 needs hood. can also use directly need specific number interpolated colours:
code: palette_names <- c(   "main", "full", "purple", "blue",   "rose", "neutral", "diverging", "light", "dark" ) n <- 8 max_label <- max(strwidth(palette_names, units = "inches", cex = 0.9)) * 1.2 par(mar = c(0.5, max_label * 5 + 1, 0.5, 0.5)) plot(NULL, xlim = c(0, n), ylim = c(0, length(palette_names)),      axes = FALSE, xlab = "", ylab = "") for (i in seq_along(palette_names)) {   nm <- palette_names[i]   cols <- rladies_pal(nm)(n)   y <- length(palette_names) - i   rect(seq(0, n - 1), y + 0.1, seq(1, n), y + 0.9, col = cols, border = NA)   mtext(nm, side = 2, at = y + 0.5, las = 1, cex = 0.9, font = 2, line = 0.5) } rladies_pal("diverging")(7) #> [1] "#6B0FD4" "#881EF9" "#BB86F7" "#EDEDF4" "#F6A4C3" "#FF5B92" "#FB80AB"

title: Getting started with spellbind
text: brand tints created mixing colour lavender (tints) charcoal (shades) — approach RLadies+ SCSS. rladies_mix() lets generate ratio: Tints (left) shades (right) purple 25% steps.
code: amounts <- seq(0.25, 1, by = 0.25) tints <- vapply(amounts, function(x) rladies_mix("purple", x, "tint"), character(1)) shades <- vapply(amounts, function(x) rladies_mix("purple", x, "shade"), character(1)) cols <- c(rev(tints), shades) par(mar = c(2, 0.5, 0.5, 0.5)) barplot(rep(1, length(cols)), col = cols, border = NA, axes = FALSE,         names.arg = c(paste0(rev(amounts) * 100, "% tint"),                       paste0(amounts * 100, "% shade")),         las = 2, cex.names = 0.7)

title: Getting started with spellbind
text: theme_rladies() minimal ggplot2 theme built theme_minimal(). uses brand’s Bastille Black text purple titles strip labels. Backgrounds transparent, plots drop cleanly slides, documents, dashboards without white rectangles fighting page colour. default light theme. dark slides coloured backgrounds, switch dark mode: Dark mode coloured dark slide backgrounds. Gridlines default. Turn grid = FALSE data speaks .
code: ggplot(diamonds, aes(cut, fill = cut)) +   geom_bar(show.legend = FALSE) +   scale_fill_rladies("full") +   theme_rladies() +   labs(title = "Diamond cuts") ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(colour = rladies_cols("purple_50"), size = 3) +   theme_rladies(mode = "dark") +   labs(title = "Dark mode")

title: Getting started with spellbind
text: spellbind ships three R Markdown templates can access RStudio’s File > New File > R Markdown > Template menu: RLadies+ HTML — branded html_document brand CSS, Poppins font, purple accents. RLadies+ PDF — branded pdf_document matching LaTeX headers. RLadies+ Xaringan — presentation slides brand colours typography. HTML PDF formats also available output functions: handle CSS LaTeX includes — just write content.
code: output: spellbind::rladies_html output: spellbind::rladies_pdf

title: Getting started with spellbind
text: typical chapter meetup workflow looks something like : complete example combining theme, scales, brand colours. palette, theme, scales pull colour definitions. Change nothing, plots look like belong together.
code: ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, colour = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_colour_rladies("main") +   theme_rladies() +   labs(     title = "Weight vs fuel efficiency",     x = "Weight (1000 lbs)",     y = "Miles per gallon",     colour = "Cylinders"   )

title: RLadies+ Brand Toolkit for R
text: spellbind RLadies+ brand toolkit R. gives colour palettes, ggplot2 theme, colour/fill scales, branded R Markdown templates everything chapter produces looks like belongs together.

title: RLadies+ Brand Toolkit for R
text: Install RLadies r-universe: development version GitHub:
code: install.packages("spellbind", repos = "https://rladies.r-universe.dev") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("rladies/spellbind")

title: RLadies+ Brand Toolkit for R
text: brand palette three primary colours — purple, blue, rose — plus neutrals tints .
code: library(spellbind) rladies_cols("purple", "blue", "rose") #>    purple      blue      rose  #> "#881ef9" "#146af9" "#ff5b92"

title: RLadies+ Brand Toolkit for R
text: theme_rladies() pairs scale_colour_rladies() scale_fill_rladies() give plots consistent branded look.  Eight palettes available — "main", "full", "purple", "blue", "rose", "neutral", "diverging", "light". See ?rladies_pal details.
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 2.5) +   scale_colour_rladies() +   theme_rladies() +   labs(title = "Iris sepal dimensions")

title: RLadies+ Brand Toolkit for R
text: Three branded templates ship package, available RStudio’s File > New File > R Markdown > Template menu: RLadies+ HTML — spellbind::rladies_html RLadies+ PDF — spellbind::rladies_pdf RLadies+ Xaringan — slide deck brand CSS

title: RLadies+ Brand Toolkit for R
text: browse pkgdown site.
code: vignette("spellbind")

title: RLadies+ brand colours — rladies_cols
text: Access individual colours full palette RLadies+ brand.

title: RLadies+ brand colours — rladies_cols
code: rladies_cols(...)

title: RLadies+ brand colours — rladies_cols
text: ... Character names colours retrieve. empty, returns colours named character vector.

title: RLadies+ brand colours — rladies_cols
text: named character vector hex colour values.

title: RLadies+ brand colours — rladies_cols
code: rladies_cols() #>         purple   purple_light      purple_50 purple_lighter    purple_dark  #>      "#881ef9"      "#a152f8"      "#bb86f7"      "#d4b9f5"      "#6b0fd4"  #>  purple_darker           blue        blue_75        blue_50        blue_25  #>      "#5009a8"      "#146af9"      "#4a8bf8"      "#80acf6"      "#b7ccf5"  #>           rose        rose_75        rose_50        rose_25       charcoal  #>      "#ff5b92"      "#fb80ab"      "#f6a4c3"      "#f2c9dc"      "#2f2f30"  #>    charcoal_75    charcoal_50    charcoal_25       lavender        surface  #>      "#5f5f61"      "#8e8e92"      "#bebec3"      "#ededf4"      "#f8f8fc"  #>    surface_alt  #>      "#f3f0fa"  rladies_cols("purple", "rose", "blue") #>    purple      rose      blue  #> "#881ef9" "#ff5b92" "#146af9"  rladies_cols("charcoal") #>  charcoal  #> "#2f2f30"

what: RLadies+ R Markdown output formats — rladies_formats
title: RLadies+ R Markdown output formats — rladies_formats
text: Output format functions creating branded RLadies+ documents. Use output field R Markdown YAML header, select RStudio's "Template" menu.

title: RLadies+ R Markdown output formats — rladies_formats
code: rladies_html(toc = TRUE, toc_float = TRUE, ...)  rladies_pdf(toc = FALSE, ...)

title: RLadies+ R Markdown output formats — rladies_formats
text: toc Logical. Include table contents? toc_float Logical. Float table contents (HTML )? ... Additional arguments passed underlying format function.

title: RLadies+ R Markdown output formats — rladies_formats
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # In YAML header: # output: spellbind::rladies_html # output: spellbind::rladies_pdf } # }

what: Tint or shade an RLadies+ brand colour — rladies_mix
title: Tint or shade an RLadies+ brand colour — rladies_mix
text: Mix brand colour lavender (tint) charcoal (shade), following RLadies+ brand guidelines. approach used brand SCSS — tints mix towards lavender white, shades mix towards Bastille Black.

title: Tint or shade an RLadies+ brand colour — rladies_mix
code: rladies_mix(colour, amount = 0.5, mode = c("tint", "shade"))

title: Tint or shade an RLadies+ brand colour — rladies_mix
text: colour colour name brand palette (e.g. "purple", "blue", "rose") hex colour string. amount Numeric 0 1. proportion original colour keep. amount = 0.75 gives 75% tint (mostly colour, lightly mixed lavender/charcoal). mode Either "tint" (mix towards lavender) "shade" (mix towards charcoal).

title: Tint or shade an RLadies+ brand colour — rladies_mix
text: hex colour string.

title: Tint or shade an RLadies+ brand colour — rladies_mix
code: rladies_mix("purple", 0.75) #> [1] "#A152F8" rladies_mix("purple", 0.50, mode = "shade") #> [1] "#5C2694"  scales::show_col(vapply(   c(0.25, 0.50, 0.75, 1),   function(x) rladies_mix("blue", x),   character(1) ))

title: RLadies+ colour palettes — rladies_pal
text: Returns colour palette function vector RLadies+ brand.

title: RLadies+ colour palettes — rladies_pal
code: rladies_pal(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: RLadies+ colour palettes — rladies_pal
text: palette Name palette. One "main", "full", "purple", "blue", "rose", "neutral", "diverging", "light". reverse Logical. Reverse palette order? ... Additional arguments passed grDevices::colorRampPalette().

title: RLadies+ colour palettes — rladies_pal
text: function takes integer n returns n interpolated colours.

title: RLadies+ colour palettes — rladies_pal
code: rladies_pal("main")(3) #> [1] "#881EF9" "#146AF9" "#FF5B92" rladies_pal("purple")(6) #> [1] "#5009A8" "#6B0FD4" "#881EF9" "#A152F8" "#BB86F7" "#D4B9F5" rladies_pal("diverging", reverse = TRUE)(7) #> [1] "#FB80AB" "#FF5B92" "#F6A4C3" "#EDEDF4" "#BB86F7" "#881EF9" "#6B0FD4"

what: RLadies+ colour and fill scales for ggplot2 — scale_rladies
title: RLadies+ colour and fill scales for ggplot2 — scale_rladies
text: Discrete continuous colour/fill scales using RLadies+ brand palettes.

title: RLadies+ colour and fill scales for ggplot2 — scale_rladies
code: scale_colour_rladies(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_rladies(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_fill_rladies(palette = "main", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_colour_rladies_c(palette = "purple", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_color_rladies_c(palette = "purple", reverse = FALSE, ...)  scale_fill_rladies_c(palette = "purple", reverse = FALSE, ...)

title: RLadies+ colour and fill scales for ggplot2 — scale_rladies
text: palette Name palette (see rladies_pal() options). reverse Logical. Reverse palette order? ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::discrete_scale() ggplot2::scale_colour_gradientn().

title: RLadies+ colour and fill scales for ggplot2 — scale_rladies
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, colour = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_colour_rladies() +   theme_rladies()   ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_rladies_c("purple") +   theme_rladies()

title: RLadies+ ggplot2 theme — theme_rladies
text: clean ggplot2 theme using RLadies+ brand palette. Supports light dark modes transparent backgrounds seamless embedding branded documents dashboards.

title: RLadies+ ggplot2 theme — theme_rladies
code: theme_rladies(base_size = 13, mode = c("light", "dark"), grid = TRUE)

title: RLadies+ ggplot2 theme — theme_rladies
text: base_size Base font size points. mode Either "light" (charcoal text transparent background) "dark" (lavender text transparent background). grid Logical. Show major gridlines?

title: RLadies+ ggplot2 theme — theme_rladies
text: ggplot2::theme() object.

title: RLadies+ ggplot2 theme — theme_rladies
code: library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(colour = rladies_cols("purple"), size = 3) +   labs(title = "Weight vs Fuel Efficiency") +   theme_rladies()   ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg)) +   geom_point(colour = rladies_cols("purple_50"), size = 3) +   labs(title = "Dark mode") +   theme_rladies(mode = "dark")
